text:
Early diagnosis of brain tumors is a very crucial factor in the treatment and survival of the patients. The tumors in the brain are always diagnosed through Biopsy after performing a major neurosurgical operation. The application of computational intelligence for the diagnosis of tumors in the brain using the MRI images and assisting the doctors in an accurate identification and classification of various abnormalities in the brain is very effective. In this research, two deep learning models and various computational intelligence methods based on machine learning algorithms are proposed and tested for the accurate identification and classification of tumors namely Glioma, Meningioma, Pituitary Tumors and Normal Brain Tissue using MRI.

In this study, a database of 3264 MRI scans was prepared. After applying certain preprocesses and data augmentation techniques to prepare the data for robust performance of the proposed model, a custom 2D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Convolutional Auto-encoder (CAE) network were designed. And the performance of the proposed models were examined with a best fit hyperparameter. The proposed 2D CNN model includes eight convolutional layers with 2x2 kernels and four pooling layers. In proposed CAE, the latent representation of the encoder is inputted to a secondary classification network. In order to investigate the performance of the proposed deep learning models and provide comparison, six conventional machine learning models have been used and the proposed models have been tested whether it supports doctors in achieving a high accuracy at early stages of cancer development.
explanation: The text uses generic, high-level research phrasing and includes minor oddities that are often seen in AI-generated summaries.
